summary: Added Completeness dimension to rubric scorer. Created prompts/completeness.md quoting rubric verbatim, documenting v1 single-page scope and the planned v2 surface-augmented oracle. Ran prepare.py (10 pending files), scored all 10 pilot pages, ran collect.py --archive and render-report.py. avg=3.4, dist 3:6/4:4 — no page scored below 3 or above 4. Common finding: code-example-only pages that lack parameter tables (send-a-message, file, image-handling, update-user-information, authentication) score 3 due to absent field-level documentation (types, constraints, return values, error cases).

score_4_pages: query-channels (3 param tables, return type documented)
score_3_pages: send-a-message (no param table — params appear only in code)
file.mdx (missing file object structure, accepted types table incomplete)
image-handling (missing image object fields, max dimensions absent)
update-user-information (missing field constraints, return type absent)
authentication (missing login() param table, userId constraints absent)
scoring_findings.dominant_anti_pattern: Code-example-only APIs — all 4 platforms show the call, but parameter names/types/constraints appear only in code snippets, not in a prose/table reference. Completeness v2 with SDK surface oracle will make this gap more precise.
